items.title: Classification and label modeling
items.description: Class labels are discrete random variables; softmax and sigmoid outputs parameterize predicted PMFs, and cross-entropy compares predicted to true label distributions.

items.title: Regression and noise modeling
items.description: Continuous targets (price, temperature, latency) are modeled with PDFs—often Gaussian—so predictions include both a point estimate and uncertainty via variance.

items.title: Generative modeling preview
items.description: VAEs, diffusion models, and autoregressive LLMs define or learn distributions over data: discrete PMFs over tokens or continuous/latent PDFs over images and embeddings.

items.title: Calibration and SLA monitoring
items.description: CDFs of predicted probabilities and latency RVs support reliability diagrams, percentile SLAs (p95/p99), and risk thresholds in production ML systems.
